summary: Crystal‑clear soundboard captures a blistering Ventura ’84 show highlighted by an epic Loser, soaring Dew and a transcendent Terrapin/Space sequence.
review: The Miller‑remastered SBD delivers pristine clarity with virtually no distortion, letting the band’s 1984 magic shine through.  Highlights include Jerry’s deep‑growl on “Loser,” Phil’s thunderous bass on “Dew,” and a soaring Terrapin Station → Drums → Space that many fans describe as cosmic.  Overall the performance feels like one of the year’s top shows, with only minor lyrical slips noted on “Terrapin.”

ai_rating.rationale: Over 30 reviews overwhelmingly praise both the musical performance and the audio fidelity; recording is deemed excellent and show quality is consistently described as outstanding.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Clear, crisp, zero distortion, superb stereo image. A few reviewers note a slightly forward hi‑hat/cymbal presence but it does not detract from overall fidelity.

poor_songs: Terrapin Station (lyric flubs/mumbling noted by a few reviewers)
show_quality.setlist_flow: The concert opens with an electrifying “Dancin’ In The Streets,” builds through tight mid‑set grooves, peaks at the Terrapin Station → Drums → Space sequence, and closes on a soaring “Brokedown Palace.” Reviewers describe the flow as seamless, energetic summer‑vibe perfection.

band_member_comments.Jerry: Praised for a deep, growling vocal on “Loser” and stellar guitar tone; a few comments mention mumbling on “Terrapin.”
band_member_comments.Phil: His bass work is highlighted as thunderous on “Dew” and “Loser,” earning the nickname ‘Thunder Lizard.’
band_member_comments.Bob: Noted for mangling lyrics on “Terrapin” by one reviewer, otherwise no major comments.
band_member_comments.Keys: Brent (keys) receives high praise for his performance of “I Just Want To Make Love To You” and overall solid playing; hi‑hat prominence is mentioned as a mix quirk.
band_member_comments.Drums: Bill/Mickey lauded for the cosmic “Drums → Space” segment and tight groove throughout.
